//: Override layer for the Web IDL bindings generator. 'skip' lists members the emitter must not generate. They keep the Starling binding. 'override' supplies a custom getter/setter the emitter uses instead of the mechanical CLR mapping. Each entry says why.

skipReason.Document.createElement: Must pick the HTML element-class prototype (HTMLDivElement and so on) so instanceof works, and apply HTML tag casing. A plain wrap of Document.CreateElement gives the generic Element prototype, so this stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.Document.createAttribute: HTML documents lowercase the attribute name and the binding validates the name. A direct call skips that, so this stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.Document.createAttributeNS: Namespace and qualified-name validation plus null-namespace handling need custom logic, so this stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.Node.cloneNode: Cloning goes through a binding-specific deep/shallow clone that rewraps the subtree with correct prototypes, so this stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.EventTarget.addEventListener: The listener registry maps JS listener object identity to host delegates and supports the handleEvent protocol and once/capture/passive options. This is coupled to the JS object model and stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.EventTarget.removeEventListener: Pairs with addEventListener's JS-identity listener registry. This stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.EventTarget.dispatchEvent: Re-enters the JS engine per listener through the event path. This is coupled to the binding event loop and stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.Element.innerHTML: Setter runs the HTML fragment-parsing algorithm and the getter runs the serializer (Starling.Html). This is coupled to the parser and stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.Element.outerHTML: Same HTML parser/serializer coupling as innerHTML. This stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.Element.insertAdjacentHTML: Runs the HTML fragment parser at a position. This stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.Document.createEvent: Legacy event factory that constructs the right Event subtype and wrapper; stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.Document.body: HTML document-tree access is already installed by NodeBindings and stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.Document.head: HTML document-tree access is already installed by NodeBindings and stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.Document.title: The Starling binding implements title lookup and setter behavior over the real title element.
skipReason.Document.getElementsByName: HTML legacy lookup returns a live NodeList and stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.Document.createElementNS: Must pick the right element-class prototype and validate the namespace and qualified name, like createElement. A plain wrap gives the generic Element prototype, so this stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.Document.createCDATASection: HTML documents reject this with NotSupportedError and the wrapper needs the CDATASection prototype, so this stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.Document.adoptNode: Adopting rewraps the moved subtree with the correct prototypes in the new document, like cloneNode, so this stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.Document.importNode: Importing clones into this document and rewraps the subtree with the correct prototypes, like cloneNode, so this stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.Document.implementation: Returns the DOMImplementation host object, which the Starling binding builds and caches, so this stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.Element.getAttributeNode: Returns an Attr (the CLR AttrNode) through the Attr-specific wrapper, so this stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.Element.getAttributeNodeNS: Returns an Attr through the Attr-specific wrapper, so this stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.Element.setAttributeNode: Takes and returns an Attr through the Attr-specific wrapper and moves the node between elements, so this stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.Element.setAttributeNodeNS: Takes and returns an Attr through the Attr-specific wrapper, so this stays with the Starling binding.
skipReason.Element.removeAttributeNode: Matches the Attr by reference and raises NotFoundError, returning the removed Attr through the Attr-specific wrapper, so this stays with the Starling binding.

//dispatch: Thin bindings that dispatch to a Starling DOM impl method (the Chromium model). The DOM impl does the spec validation and raises a typed DomException, which the binding translates.

Node.nodeType.//: The CLR node discriminator is the NodeKind enum (named Kind), not a numeric NodeType property; expose its integer DOM spec code, which is what nodeType returns.

Node.childNodes.//: Starling returns a real JS Array of children (like querySelectorAll).

Element.children.getter: (thisV, _) => DomWrappers.UnwrapAs<Element>(thisV) is { } e ? IdlMarshal.WrapHtmlCollection(realm, () => e.ChildElements()) : JsValue.Null

Document.children.getter: (thisV, _) => DomWrappers.UnwrapAs<Document>(thisV) is { } d ? IdlMarshal.WrapHtmlCollection(realm, () => d.ChildElements()) : JsValue.Null

Element.classList.//: Cache the DOMTokenList wrapper on the JS element object so el.classList === el.classList.
Element.classList.getter: (thisV, _) => { if (DomWrappers.UnwrapElement(thisV) is not { } e || !thisV.IsObject) return JsValue.Null; var w = thisV.AsObject; var cached = w.Get("__classList__"); if (!cached.IsUndefined) return cached; var o = JsValue.Object(NodeBindings.BuildDomTokenList(realm, e)); w.Set("__classList__", o); return o; }

Element.attributes.//: Cache the NamedNodeMap wrapper on the JS element object for stable identity.
Element.attributes.getter: (thisV, _) => { if (DomWrappers.UnwrapElement(thisV) is not { } e || !thisV.IsObject) return JsValue.Null; var w = thisV.AsObject; var cached = w.Get("__attributes__"); if (!cached.IsUndefined) return cached; var o = JsValue.Object(new JsNamedNodeMapObject(realm, e)); w.Set("__attributes__", o); return o; }

Node.nodeName.//: HTML uppercases an element's node name and special nodes have fixed names. Reuse the Starling NormalizeNodeName helper.

Element.tagName.//: ASCII-uppercase the tag name only for HTML-namespace elements (DOM 4.9).
Element.tagName.getter: (thisV, _) => DomWrappers.UnwrapAs<Element>(thisV) is { } h ? JsValue.String(h.Namespace == Element.HtmlNamespace ? h.TagName.ToUpperInvariant() : h.TagName) : JsValue.String("")

Element.namespaceURI.//: The CLR Element.Namespace is a non-null string ('' for the null namespace); namespaceURI returns null in that case (DOM 4.9).
Element.namespaceURI.getter: (thisV, _) => DomWrappers.UnwrapAs<Element>(thisV) is { } h ? IdlMarshal.WrapString(string.IsNullOrEmpty(h.Namespace) ? null : h.Namespace) : JsValue.Null
